frozen_components: Transformer backbone (implied frozen during MFN training)
architecture.summary: Monosemantic Feature Neurons (MFNs) are a sparse autoencoding layer embedded inside Transformer blocks. The system encodes residual streams into K-sparse codes via an encoder network, reconstructs them via a decoder, and blends the reconstructed sparse representation back into the residual stream for downstream computation. Information flows: residual stream → encoder → K-sparse bottleneck → decoder → blend-and-residual-add → next layer. Five loss terms (reconstruction, sparsity, competition, stability, utility) jointly train the encoder and decoder to produce features biased toward functional monosemanticity.
trained_components: encoder network (residual stream to K-sparse codes)

biblio.title: Monosemantic Feature Neurons: A Sparse Autoencoding Layer for Interpretable, Steerable Transformer Features

claims.statement: MFNs encode residual streams into K-sparse codes, reconstruct them, and blend them back into the model so that downstream computation depends on a sparse, interpretable basis
Five complementary loss terms (reconstruction, sparsity, competition, stability, and utility) bias features toward functional monosemanticity
Unlike post-hoc sparse autoencoders, MFNs embed a causally entangled bottleneck directly within Transformer computation, bridging the gap between interpretability and train-time transparency
The work outlines falsifiable predictions, evaluation metrics, and practical training heuristics for future empirical validation
